name: Failure modes and effects analysis

problemSignals.description: A design or process change introduces uncertain failure modes.
Component or process-step failures need systematic prioritization.

requiredEvidence.description: Bounded functions, requirements, interfaces, and operating conditions.
Observed failures, analogous history, tests, or justified expert elicitation.

assumptions: Severity, occurrence, and detection scales are defined before scoring

deterministicSteps.action: Enumerate bounded functions and requirements at one declared analysis level.
For each function enumerate failure modes, effects, causes, controls, and evidence source.
Apply the declared ordinal scales and rank recommended risk-reduction actions without treating rank as observed probability.

outputs.description: Declared FMEA scope and analysis level.
Traceable modes, effects, causes, controls, and evidence.
Prioritized proposed controls with scale limitations.

whenAll: Failure modes have operational or test evidence
Some occurrence or detection ratings rely on expert judgment

contraindications: A single known top event is the only analysis target
Scores would be used as precise probabilities without calibration

contract: Keep rating scales and evidence explicit

source.title: IEC 60812 Failure modes and effects analysis
